name: My Favorite Cobbler
recipeInstructions:
1. Any kind of fruit or berries can be used. My favorite is black or boysenberries, freshly picked. Wash the berries in cold water.
2. Oil the pot and put about 1/2 inch of water in the bottom.
3. Sweeten the berries to taste, then sweeten a little more. Some berries taste sweet enough fresh off the vine but in pies or cobblers they come out too tart, so be generous with sweeteners.
4. Pour the fruit into the pot. Use a yellow cake mix, but I like a lemon or butterscotch for variety. Mix the cake mix according to directions and stir in a generous amount of walnuts.
5. Since moving to Hurricane, pecans have taken the place of walnuts at times.
6. Spread evenly over the fruit.
7. Cook with low heat on the bottom, higher on top. Check often.
